item.title: Anchoring Bias: How the First Number You See Controls Your Wallet
item.description: You walk into a store and see a jacket priced at $500. You keep browsing and find another jacket for $200. It feels like a bargain, right? But step back for a moment.

item.title: The Hidden Costs of Sleep Deprivation Your Doctor Won't Tell You
item.description: You probably know that sleep deprivation is bad for you. But what most people underestimate is just how far the damage extends beyond feeling tired. The research paints a picture that should genuinely alarm anyone who routinely gets less than seven hours of sleep.

item.title: Why the Smartest People Make the Worst Financial Decisions
item.description: There is a comforting assumption most people carry around: the smarter you are, the better your decisions. It makes intuitive sense. If you can solve complex problems, analyze data, and think abstractly, surely you can navigate personal finance better than someone who cannot.

item.title: About Clear Signals
item.description: Clear Signals is an evidence-based publication exploring the hidden forces behind your decisions about money, health, and the world around you. Why “Clear Signals”? Every day, you’re bombarded with noise: clickbait headlines, contradictory advice, opinions dressed up as facts.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description: Last updated: March 25, 2026 Clear Signals (“we,” “us,” or “our”) operates the website clearsignals.cloud. This page explains how we collect, use, and protect information when you use the site.

item.title: You're In
item.description: Thanks for subscribing. You will receive evidence-based insights on money, health, and decision-making delivered to your inbox every week. No spam. No fluff. Just research-backed clarity. Back to the latest articles →
